The most accomplished Battle Masters are well-rounded figures who combine their carefully honed combat skills with academic study in the fields of history, theory, and the arts.

children.name: Student of War Skill Proficiency
children.target: acrobaticsProficiency OR animalHandlingProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR historyProficiency OR insightProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR persuasionProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR survivalProficiency

children.name: Student of War Tool Proficiency
children.target: tool NOT gamingSet NOT musicalInstrument NOT thievesTools NOT poisonersKit NOT navigatorsTools NOT herbalismKit NOT forgeryKit NOT disguiseKit OR artisansToolsProficiency

children.name: Know Your Enemy
children.description: You discern certain strengths and weaknesses of a creature you can see within range. You know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are.

children.name: Relentless
children.description: You can roll 1d8 and use the number rolled instead of expending a Superiority Die.

name: Champion
description: A Champion focuses on the development of martial prowess in a relentless pursuit of victory. Champions combine rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devastating blows, withstand peril, and garner glory. Whether in athletic contests or bloody battle, Champions strive for the crown of the victor.

children.name: Improved Critical
children.description: You score a critical hit on a roll of $(meta.level >= 15 ? '18-20' : '19 or 20').

children.name: Remarkable Athlete
children.description: On a critical hit, you can move up to $(attributes.movement.walk/2) feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

children.name: Heroic Warrior
children.description: The thrill of battle drives you toward victory. During combat, you can give yourself Heroic Inspiration whenever you start your turn without it.

children.name: Heroic Rally
children.description: At the start of each of your turns, you regain $(5 + stats.con.mod) Hit Points if you are Bloodied and have at least 1 Hit Point.

name: Eldritch Knight
description: Eldritch Knights combine the martial mastery common to all Fighters with a careful study of magic. Their spells both complement and extend their combat skills, providing additional protection to shore up their armor and also allowing them to engage many foes at once with explosive magic.

children.name: Eldritch Knight Spells
children.target: wizard NOT class NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

children.name: War Bond
children.description: You summon your bonded weapon, causing it to teleport instantly to your hand. You can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

children.name: War Magic
children.description: You can replace one of the attacks with a cantrip.

children.name: Eldritch Strike
children.description: On a hit, the target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

children.name: Arcane Charge
children.description: You can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

children.name: Improved War Magic
children.description: You can replace two of the attacks with a level 1 or level 2 spell.

name: Psi Warrior
description: Psi Warriors awaken the power of their minds to augment their physical might. They harness this psionic power to infuse their weapon strikes, lash out with telekinetic energy, and create barriers of mental force.

children.name: Protective Field
children.description: When you or another creature you can see within range takes damage, you create a momentary shield of telekinetic force. Reduce the damage taken by 1d$(attributes.psiDice)$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)).

children.name: Psionic Strike
children.description: When you hit a target with a weapon attack, you propel your weapon with psionic force. The target takes 1d$(attributes.psiDice)$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)) Force damage.

children.name: Telekinetic Movement
children.description: You move an object or creature with your mind. Choose one target you can see within range; the target must be a loose object that is Large or smaller, or one willing creature other than you. You transport the target up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, if the target is a Tiny object, you can transport it to or from your hand.

children.name: Psi Powered Leap
children.description: You gain a Fly Speed of $(attributes.movement.walk * 2) feet until the end of the current turn.

children.name: Telekinetic Thrust
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(8+ attributes.prof + stats.int.mod) Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you can give the target the Prone condition or transport it up to 10 feet horizontally.

children.name: Guarded Mind
children.description: When you start your turn with the Charmed or Frightened condition, you end every effect on yourself giving you those conditions.

children.name: Bulwark of Force
children.description: You shield yourself and others with telekinetic force. Choose $(Math.max(1, stats.int.mod)) creatures within range. Each of the chosen creatures has Half Cover for 1 minute or until you have the incapacitated condition.

children.name: Telekinetic Master
children.description: Until the spell ends, including the turn when you cast it, you can make one attack with a weapon as a Bonus Action.

name: Warrior of the Elements
description: Warriors of the Elements tap into the power of the Elemental Planes. Harnessing their supernatural focus, these Monks momentarily tame the energy of the Elemental Chaos to empower themselves in and out of battle.

children.name: Elemental Attunement
children.description:
You imbue yourself with elemental energy. While active, gain the following benefits:
**Reach** Your Unarmed Strike reach is 10 feet greater than normal, as elemental energy extends from you.

**Elemental Strikes** You can cause Unarmed Strikes to deal your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage. When you deal one of these types, force the target to make a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Strength saving throw. On a failure, move the target up to 10 feet toward or away from you."

children.name: Elemental Burst
children.description: You cause elemental energy to burst in a 20-foot-radius Sphere. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Each creature in the Sphere must make a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d$(attributes.martialArtsDie) damage of the chosen type on a failure or half as much on a success.

children.name: Stride of the Elements
children.description: You have a Fly Speed and a Swim Speed of $(attributes.movement.walk) feet.

children.name: Elemental Epitome
children.description: You gain Resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

children.name: Destructive Stride
children.description: When you use Step of the Wind, your Speed increases by 20 feet until the end of the turn. Any creature of your choice takes 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie) damage (Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder; your choice) when you enter a space within 5 feet of it. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

children.name: Empowered Elemental Strikes
children.description: Once on each of your turns, you can deal 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie) extra damage when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike.

name: Warrior of Mercy
description: Warriors of Mercy manipulate the life force of others. These Monks are wandering physicians, but they bring a swift end to their enemies. They often wear masks, presenting themselves as faceless bringers of life and death.

children.name: Hand of Harm
children.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you deal 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie)+$(stats.wis.mod) extra Necrotic damage.$(meta.level >= 6 ? ' You can also give the target the Poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.' : '')

children.name: Hand of Healing
children.description: You touch a creature and restore 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie)+$(stats.wis.mod) Hit Points to it.$(meta.level >= 6 ? ' You can also end one condition on the creature: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned.' : '')

children.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
children.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace each Unarmed Strike with Hand of Healing without expending Focus Points. When you make an Unarmed Strike with Flurry of Blows and deal damage, you can use Hand of Harm without expending a Focus Point.

children.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
children.description:
You touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours. The creature returns to life with 4d10+$(stats.wis.mod) Hit Points, with the following conditions removed: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, and Stunned.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

name: Warrior of the Open Hand
description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents and manipulate their own energy to protect themselves from harm.

children.name: Open Hand Technique
children.description: When you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you impose one of the following effects on the target.

children.name: Addle
children.description: The target can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.

children.name: Push
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you.

children.name: Topple
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition.

children.name: Wholeness of Body
children.description: You channel your inner healing energy. Regain 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie)+$(stats.wis.mod) Hit Points.

children.name: Fleet Step
children.description: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that Bonus Action.

children.name: Quivering Palm
children.description: When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you start imperceptible vibrations lasting $(meta.level) days. As an action or by forgoing one Attack action strike, end the vibrations: the target must make a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Constitution saving throw, taking 10d12 Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. You can have only one creature under this effect at a time.

name: Warrior of Shadow
description: Warriors of Shadow practice stealth and subterfuge, harnessing the power of the Shadowfell. They are at home in darkness, able to draw gloom around themselves to hide, leap from shadow to shadow, and take on a wraithlike form.

overwrite.extra: **Shadow Arts** You can see within the spell's area. While the spell persists, you can move its area of Darkness to a space within 60 feet of yourself at the start of each of your turns.

children.name: Shadow Step
children.description: While entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, you teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in Dim Light or Darkness. You then have Advantage on the next melee attack you make before the end of the current turn.

children.name: Improved Shadow Step
children.description: Expend 1 Focus Point to remove the Dim Light or Darkness requirement for this use. As part of this Bonus Action, make an Unarmed Strike immediately after you teleport.

children.name: Cloak of Shadows
children.description: While entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, you shroud yourself with shadows for 1 minute, until you have the Incapacitated condition, or until you end your turn in Bright Light.

children.name: Invisibility
children.description: You have the Invisible condition.

children.name: Partially Incorporeal
children.description: You can move through occupied spaces as if they were Difficult Terrain. If you end your turn in such a space, you are shunted to the last unoccupied space you were in.

children.name: Shadow Flurry
children.description: You can use your Flurry of Blows without expending any Focus Points.

name: Oath of Devotion
description: The Oath of Devotion binds Paladins to the ideals of Justice and Order. These Paladins meet the archetype of the knight in shining armor.

children.name: Sacred Weapon
children.description:
When you take the Attack action, you imbue one Melee weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For the duration or until you use this feature again, you add $(formatBonus(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod))) to attack rolls you make with that weapon, and each time you hit with it, you cause it to deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. 

The weapon also emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light 20 feet beyond that. You can end this effect early (no action required). This effect also ends if you aren't carrying the weapon.

children.name: Aura of Devotion
children.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the Charmed condition. If a Charmed ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there.

children.name: Smite of Protection
children.description: You and your allies have Half Cover while in your Aura of Protection. The aura has this benefit until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Holy Nimbus
children.description: You imbue your Aura of Protection with holy power, granting the benefits below for the duration or until you end them (no action required):

children.name: Holy Ward
children.description: You have Advantage on any saving throw you are forced to make by a Fiend or an Undead.

children.name: Radiant Damage
children.description: Whenever an enemy starts its turn in the aura, that creature takes $(stats.cha.mod + attributes.prof) Radiant damage.

children.name: Sunlight
children.description: The aura is filled with Bright Light that is sunlight.

name: Oath of Glory
description: Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism.

children.name: Inspiring Smite
children.description: Immediately after you cast Divine Smite, you distribute Temporary Hit Points to creatures of your choice within range, which can include you. The total number of Temporary Hit Points equals 2d8$(formatBonus(meta.level)), divided among the chosen creatures however you like.

children.name: Peerless Athlete
children.description: You augment your athleticism. For the duration, you have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks, and the distance of your Long and High Jumps increases by 10 feet (this extra distance costs movement as normal).

children.name: Aura of Alacrity
children.description: Whenever an ally enters the aura for the first time on a turn or starts their turn there, the ally's Speed increases by 10 feet until the end of their next turn.

children.name: Glorious Defense
children.description: When you or another creature you can see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you turn defense into a sudden strike. Grant a $(formatBonus(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod))) bonus to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. If the attack misses, you can make one attack with a weapon against the attacker as part of this Reaction if the attacker is within your weapon's range.

children.name: Living Legend
children.description: You empower yourself with the legends of your great deeds. Gain the benefits below for the duration:

children.name: Charismatic
children.description: You are blessed with an otherworldly presence and have Advantage on all Charisma checks.

children.name: Saving Throw Reroll
children.description: If you fail a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to reroll it. You must use this new roll.

children.name: Unerring Strike
children.description: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack roll with a weapon and miss, you can cause that attack to hit instead.

name: Oath of the Ancients
description: Paladins who swear this oath cherish the light; they love the beautiful and life-giving things of the world more than any principles of honor, courage, and justice.

children.name: Nature's Wrath
children.description: You conjure spectral vines around nearby creatures. Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Strength saving throw or have the Restrained condition for 1 minute. A Restrained creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

children.name: Aura of Warding
children.description: Ancient magic lies so heavily upon you that it forms an eldritch ward, blunting energy from beyond the Material Plane. You and your allies have Resistance to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.

children.name: Undying Sentinel
children.description: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points and not killed outright, you drop to 1 Hit Point instead, and regain $(meta.level * 3) Hit Points. Additionally, you can't be aged magically, and you cease visibly aging.

children.name: Elder Champion
children.description: You imbue your Aura of Protection with primal power, granting the benefits below for the duration or until you end them (no action required):

children.name: Diminish Defiance
children.description: Enemies in the aura have Disadvantage on saving throws against your spells and Channel Divinity options.

children.name: Regeneration
children.description: At the start of each of your turns, you regain 10 Hit Points.

children.name: Swift Spells
children.description: Whenever you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can cast it using a Bonus Action instead.

name: Oath of the Noble Genies
description: Paladins sworn to the Oath of the Noble Genies revere the forces of the Elemental Planes. Through taking this oath, Paladins draw power from the four different types of genies.

children.name: Elemental Smite
children.description: Immediately after you cast Divine Smite, you invoke one of the following effects.

children.name: Dao's Crush
children.description: Earth rises up around the target of your Divine Smite. The target has the Grappled condition (escape DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save)). While Grappled, the target has the Restrained condition.

children.name: Djinni's Escape
children.description: You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself and take on a semi-incorporeal form, which lasts until the end of your next turn. While in this form, you have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and you have Immunity to the Grappled, Prone, and Restrained conditions.

children.name: Efreeti's Fury
children.description: The target of your Divine Smite takes an extra 2d4 Fire damage, and fire jumps from the target to another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The second creature also takes 2d4 Fire damage.

children.name: Marid's Surge
children.description: The target of your Divine Smite and each creature of your choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature is pushed 15 feet straight away from you and has the Prone condition.

children.name: Genie's Splendor Skill
children.target: acrobaticsProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR performanceProficiency OR persuasionProficiency

children.name: Aura of Elemental Shielding
children.description: Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. You and your allies have Resistance to that damage type. At the start of each of your turns, you can change the damage type affected by this feature to one of the other listed options (no action required).

children.name: Elemental Rebuke
children.description: When you are hit by an attack roll, you halve the attack's damage against yourself (round down) and force the attacker to make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the attacker takes 2d10$(formatBonus(stats.cha.mod)) damage of one of the following types (your choice): Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. On a success, the attacker takes half as much damage.

children.name: Noble Scion
children.description: You gain the benefits below for the duration or until you end them (no action required):

children.name: Flight
children.description: You have a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover.

children.name: Minor Wish
children.description: When you or an ally in your Aura of Protection fails a D20 Test, you can take a Reaction to make you or that ally succeed instead.

name: Oath of Vengeance
description: The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed grievously evil acts.

children.name: Vow of Enmity
children.description: When you take the Attack action, you utter a vow of enmity against a creature you can see within range. You have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature for the duration or until you use this feature again. If the creature drops to 0 Hit Points before the vow ends, you can transfer the vow to a different creature within 30 feet of yourself (no action required).

children.name: Relentless Avenger
children.description: Your supernatural focus helps you close off a foe's retreat. When you hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack, you can reduce the creature's Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn. You can then move up to half your Speed as part of the same Reaction. This movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks.

children.name: Soul of Vengeance
children.description: Immediately after a creature under the effect of your Vow of Enmity hits or misses with an attack roll, you make a melee attack against that creature if it's within range.

children.name: Avenging Angel
children.description: You gain the benefits below for the duration or until you end them (no action required):

children.name: Flight
children.description: You sprout spectral wings on your back and have a Fly Speed of 60 feet, and can hover.

children.name: Frightful Aura
children.description: Whenever an enemy starts its turn in your Aura of Protection, that creature must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or have the Frightened condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. Attack rolls against the Frightened creature have Advantage.

name: Beast Master
description: A Beast Master forms a mystical bond with a special animal, drawing on primal magic and a deep connection to the natural world.

children.name: Command Companion
children.description: You can replace one attack with a command to your Primal Companion beast to take the Beast's Strike action$(meta.level >= 11 ? ' twice' : '').

children.name: Command Companion
children.description: You command your Primal Companion beast to take an action. The beast acts during your turn and can move and use its Reaction on its own, but only takes the Dodge action unless you command it.

children.name: Exceptional Training
children.description: You can also command the beast to take the Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help action using its Bonus Action. Whenever it hits with an attack roll and deals damage, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

children.name: Bestial Fury
children.description: When you command your beast to take the Beast's Strike action, the beast can use it twice. The first time each turn it hits a creature under the effect of your Hunter's Mark spell, the beast deals extra Force damage equal to the bonus damage of that spell.

children.name: Share Spells
children.target: activities[(cantrip OR level1Spell OR level2Spell OR level3Spell OR level4Spell OR level5Spell OR level6Spell OR level7Spell OR level8Spell OR level9Spell) AND range=self]
children.description: You can also affect your Primal Companion beast with the spell if the beast is within 30 feet of you.

name: Fey Wanderer
description: A fey mystique surrounds you, thanks to the boon of an archfey or a location in the Feywild that transformed you. Your joyful laughter brightens the hearts of the downtrodden, and your martial prowess strikes terror in your foes, for great is the mirth of the fey and dreadful is their fury.

children.name: Dreadful Strikes
children.description: On a hit, once per turn, deal an extra $(meta.level >= 11 ? '1d6' : '1d4') Psychic damage to the target.

children.name: Beguiling Twist
children.description: When you or a creature you can see within 120 feet succeeds on a saving throw to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition, you force a different creature you can see within 120 feet to make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom save. On a failure, the target is Charmed or Frightened (your choice) for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

overwrite.extra: **Fey Reinforcements** You can cast this without a Material component. Whenever you start casting it, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute.

**Misty Wanderer.** You can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of your destination space.

name: Gloom Stalker
description: Gloom Stalkers are at home in the darkest places, wielding magic drawn from the Shadowfell to combat enemies that lurk in darkness.

children.name: Ambusher's Leap
children.description: At the start of your first turn of each combat, your speed increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn.

children.name: Dreadful Strike
children.description: Once per turn when you attack a creature and hit it with a weapon, you deal an extra $(meta.level >= 11 ? '2d8' : '2d6') Psychic damage. $(meta.level >= 11 ? 'Additionally, you can cause one of the following effects:' : '')

children.name: Umbral Sight
children.description: While entirely in Darkness, you have the Invisible condition to any creature that relies on Darkvision to see you in that Darkness.

children.name: Sudden Strike
children.description: Make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within the weapon's range.

children.name: Mass Fear
children.description: The target and each creature within 10 feet of it must make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature has the Frightened condition until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Shadowy Dodge
children.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you impose Disadvantage on that roll. Whether the attack hits or misses, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see.

name: Hunter
description: You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities as a Hunter to protect nature and people everywhere from forces that would destroy them.

children.name: Hunter's Lore
children.description: You know whether the creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if it has any, you know what they are.

children.name: Superior Hunter's Prey
children.description: Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by this spell, you can also deal that spell's extra damage to a different creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature.

children.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
children.description: When you take damage, you give yourself Resistance to that damage and any other damage of the same type until the end of the current turn.

name: Winter Walker
description: Winter Walkers hone their craft in the bleak and frozen wilds of places like Icewind Dale. These ruthless, rimed Rangers hunt monsters that haunt arctic wastelands, eventually becoming frigid terrors themselves.

children.name: Biting Cold
children.target: (cantrip OR level1Spell OR level2Spell OR level3Spell OR level4Spell OR level5Spell OR level6Spell OR level7Spell OR level8Spell OR level9Spell) AND coldDamage
children.description: This damage ignores Resistance to Cold damage.

children.name: Polar Strikes
children.description: On a hit, once per turn, deal an extra $(meta.level >= 11 ? '1d6' : '1d4') Cold damage to the target. This damage ignores Resistances.

children.name: Hunter's Rime
children.description: When you cast this spell, gain 1d10+$(meta.level) Temporary Hit Points. While a creature is marked by this spell, it can't take the Disengage action.

children.name: Fortifying Soul
children.description: You choose $(Math.max(1, stats.wis.mod)) creatures you can see. Each chosen creature regains 1d10+$(meta.level) Hit Points and has Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition for 1 hour.

children.name: Chilling Retribution
children.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, the creature makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target has the Stunned condition until the end of your next turn. While Stunned, its Speed is reduced to 0 feet.

children.name: Frozen Haunt
children.description:
When you cast Hunter's Mark, you adopt a ghostly, snowy form. This form lasts until the spell ends. While you are in this form, you gain the following benefits:

**Frozen Soul.** You have Immunity to Cold damage. When you first adopt this form and at the start of each of your subsequent turns, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot Emanation originating from you takes 2d4 Cold damage. 

**Partially Incorporeal.** You have Immunity to the Grappled, Prone, and Restrained conditions. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, but you take 1d10 Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object."

name: Arcane Trickster
description: Some Rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with spells, learning magical tricks to aid them in their trade. Some Arcane Tricksters use their talents as pickpockets and burglars, while others are pranksters.

children.name: Arcane Trickster Spells
children.target: wizard NOT class NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

children.name: Magical Ambush
children.description: If you have the Invisible condition when you cast this spell, the target has Disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against the spell.

children.value: or have the Prone condition. You can also use this option on another creature within 5 feet ofyour Mage Hand.

children.name: Spell Thief
children.description: Immediately after a creature casts a spell that targets you or includes you in its area of effect, the target makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, you negate the spell's effect against you, and you steal the knowledge of the spell if it is at least level 1 and of a level you can cast. For the next 8 hours, you have the spell prepared. The creature can't cast it until 8 hours have passed.

name: Assassin
description: An Assassin's training focuses on using stealth, poison, and disguise to eliminate foes with deadly efficiency. While some Rogues who follow this path are hired killers, spies, or bounty hunters, the capabilities of this subclass are equally useful for adventurers facing a variety of monstrous enemies.

children.name: Surprising Strike
children.description: On a hit during the first round of combat, the target takes $(meta.level) extra damage.

children.name: Surprising Strike
children.description: During the first round of each combat, you have Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn.

children.name: Masterful Mimicry
children.description: You unerringly mimic another person's speech, handwriting, or both if you have spent at least 1 hour studying them.

children.name: Roving Aim
children.description: Your Speed isn't reduced to 0 by using Steady Aim.

children.target: activities[cunningStrike].extra["have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute"]
children.value: take 2d6 Poison damage and have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute

children.name: Death Strike
children.description: On a hit during the first round of a combat, the target must succeed on a DC $(8 + stats.dex.mod + attributes.prof) Constitution saving throw, or the attack's damage is doubled.

name: Scion of the Three
description: A Scion of the Three draws power from a group of malevolent gods known in Baldur's Gate as the Dead Three: Bane, a god of tyranny; Bhaal, a god of violence and murder; and Myrkul, a god of death. While some Rogues of this subclass pledge themselves ardently to those three macabre gods, others are thrust on this path by a curse. Either way, a scion's power manifests as various occult gifts, as well as an uncanny talent for striking and terrifying foes.

children.name: Bloodthirst
children.description: When an enemy you can see within range takes damage and is Bloodied after taking that damage but not killed outright, you teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of that enemy. You can then make one melee attack.

children.name: Terrify (Cost: 1d6)
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.cunningStrikeSave) Wisdom saving throw, or it has the Frightened condition for 1 minute. While the target is Frightened in this way, you have Advantage on attack rolls against the target. The Frightened target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

children.name: Aura of Malevolence
children.description: Each creature of your choice within 10 feet of either the space you left or your destination space (your choice) takes $(stats.int.mod) damage. Damage dealt by this feature ignores Resistance.

children.name: Murderous Intent
children.description: You can treat a roll of 1 or 2 on the die as a 3.

children.name: Cutthroat
children.description: Restore a Bloodthirst charge.

name: Soulknife
description: A Soulknife strikes with the mind, cutting through barriers both physical and psychic. These Rogues discover psionic power within themselves and channel it to do their roguish work. As a Soulknife your psionic abilities might have haunted you since childhood, revealing their full potential only as you experienced the stress of adventure. Or you might have sought out an order of psychic adepts and spent years learning how to manifest your power.

children.name: Psi-Bolstered Knack
children.description: When you fail an ability check using a skill or tool with which you have proficiency, you add 1d$(attributes.psiDice) to the check, potentially turning failure into success. The die is expended only if the roll then succeeds.

children.name: Psychic Whispers
children.description: You choose up to $(attributes.prof) creatures you can see. For 1d$(attributes.psiDice) hours, the chosen creatures can speak telepathically to you, and you can speak telepathically with them. To send or receive a message (no action required), you and the other creature must be within 1 mile of each other. A creature can end the telepathic connection at any time (no action required).

children.name: Attack: Psychic Blade
children.description:
You manifest a shimmering blade of psychic energy and strike at a target within range. Make a $(formatBonus(stats.dex.mod + attributes.prof)) attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d6$(formatBonus(stats.dex.mod)) Psychic damage. The blade vanishes immediately after it hits or misses, leaving no mark.

After you attack with the blade on your turn, you can make a melee or ranged attack with a second psychic blade as a Bonus Action on the same turn. The damage die of this bonus attack is 1d4 instead of 1d6.

children.name: Homing Strikes
children.description: If you miss with your Psychic Blade, you add 1d$(attributes.psiDice) to the attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, the die is expended.

children.name: Psychic Teleportation
children.description: You manifest and throw a Psychic Blade at an unoccupied space you can see up to a number of feet away equal to 10 times 1d$(attributes.psiDice). You then teleport to that space, and the blade vanishes.

children.name: Psychic Veil
children.description: You gain the Invisible condition for 1 hour or until you dismiss the effect (no action required). This invisibility ends early immediately after you deal damage to a creature or you force a creature to make a saving throw.

children.name: Rend Mind
children.description: When you use your Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage, the target makes a DC $(8 + stats.dex.mod + attributes.prof) Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition for 1 minute. The Stunned target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

name: Thief
description: A mix of burglar, treasure hunter, and explorer, you are the epitome of an adventurer. In addition to improving your agility and stealth, you gain abilities useful for delving into ruins and getting maximum benefit from the magic items you find there.

children.name: Fast Hands
children.description:
You do one of the following.

**Sleight of Hand.** Make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check to pick a lock or disarm a trap with Thieves' Tools or to pick a pocket.

**Use an Object.** Take the Utilize action, or take the Magic action to use a magic item that requires an action.

children.name: Stealth Attack (Cost: 1d6)
children.description: If you have the Hide action's Invisible condition, this attack doesn't end that condition on you if you end the turn behind Three-Quarters Cover or Total Cover.

children.name: Thief's Reflexes
children.description: You take two turns during the first round of any combat. Take your first turn at your normal Initiative and your second turn at your Initiative minus 10.

name: Aberrant Sorcery
description: An alien influence has wrapped its tendrils around your mind, giving you psionic power. You can now touch other minds with that power and alter the world around you. Will this power shine from you as a hopeful beacon to others? Or will you be a terror to those who feel the stab of your mind?

children.name: Telepathic Speech
children.description: You form a telepathic connection with one creature you can see. You can communicate while within $(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod)) miles of each other.

children.name: Revelation in Flesh
children.description:
You unleash the aberrant truth hidden within yourself. For the duration and for each Sorcery Point you expend, you gain one of the following benefits of your choice:

**Aquatic Adaptation.** You gain a Swim Speed of $(attributes.movement.walk * 2) feet, and you can breathe underwater. Gills grow from your neck or flare behind your ears, and your fingers become webbed or you grow wriggling cilia.

**Glistening Flight.** You gain a Fly Speed of $(attributes.movement.walk) feet, and you can hover. As you fly, your skin glistens with mucus or otherworldly light.

**See the Invisible.** You can see any Invisible creature within 60 feet of yourself that isn’t behind Total Cover. Your eyes also turn black or become writhing sensory tendrils.

**Wormlike Movement.** Your body, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, becomes slimy and pliable. You can move through any space as narrow as 1 inch, and you can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or the Grappled condition.

children.name: Warping Implosion
children.description: You unleash a space-warping anomaly. You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within range. Each creature within 30 feet of your previous space makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Strength saving throw.  On a failed save, a creature takes 3d10 Force damage and is pulled straight toward the space you left, ending in an unoccupied space as close to your former space as possible. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

name: Clockwork Sorcery
description: The cosmic force of order has suffused you with magic. That power arises from Mechanus or a realm like it—a plane of existence shaped entirely by clockwork efficiency. You or someone from your lineage might have become entangled in the machinations of modrons, the orderly beings who inhabit Mechanus.

children.name: Restore Balance
children.description: When a creature you can see rolls with Advantage or Disadvantage, you prevent the roll from being affected by it.

children.name: Bastion of Law
children.description:
You tap into the grand equation of existence to imbue a creature with a shimmering shield of order. Create a magical ward around a creature within range. The ward is represented by a number of d8s equal to the number of Sorcery Points spent to create it. When the warded creature takes damage, it can expend a number of those dice, roll them, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled on those dice.

The ward lasts until you finish a Long Rest or until you use this feature again.

children.name: Trance of Order
children.description: You align your consciousness with the endless calculations of Mechanus. For the duration, attack rolls against you can’t benefit from Advantage, and whenever you make a D20 Test, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.

children.name: Clockwork Cavalcade
children.description:
You momentarily summon spirits of order to expunge disorder around you. You summon the spirits in a 30-foot Cube originating from you. The spirits look like modrons or other Constructs of your choice. The spirits are intangible and invulnerable, and they create the effects below within the Cube before vanishing:

**Heal**: The spirits restore up to 100 Hit Points, divided as you choose among any number of creatures of your choice in the Cube.

**Repair**: Any damaged objects entirely in the Cube are repaired instantly.

**Dispel**: Every spell of level 6 and lower ends on creatures and objects of your choice in the Cube.

name: Draconic Sorcery
description: Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon facing death bequeathed some of its magical power to you or your ancestor. You might have absorbed magic from a site infused with dragons’ power. Or perhaps you handled a treasure taken from a dragon’s hoard that was steeped in draconic power.

children.name: Dragon Wings
children.description: You cause draconic wings to appear on your back. The wings last for the duration or until you dismiss them (no action required). For the duration, you have a Fly Speed of 60 feet.

children.name: Dragon Companion
children.description: You can cast this spell without expending a spell lost once per Long Rest. Whenever you start casting this spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.

name: Spellfire Sorcery
description: Your innate power stems from the source of magic itself: the Weave. This connection manifests as a rare ability known as spellfire, and you surge with radiant bursts of this raw magic. Your talent with spellfire allows you to heal allies, sear enemies, and absorb powerful spells.

children.name: Spellfire Burst
children.description:
When you spend at least 1 Sorcery Point as part of a Magic action or a Bonus Action on your turn, you unleash one of the following magical effects of your choice. You can do so only once per turn.

**Bolstering Flames**: You or one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself gains 1d$(meta.level >= 14 ? 8 : 4)$(formatBonus(stats.cha.mod)) Temporary Hit Points.

**Radiant Fire**: One creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself takes 1d$(meta.level >= 14 ? 8 : 4)$(formatBonus(stats.cha.mod)) Fire or Radiant damage (your choice).

children.name: Absorb Spells
children.description: When the target fails the save, you regain 1d4 Sorcery Points.

children.name: Crown of Spellfire
children.description:
When you use Innate Sorcery, you alter it and infuse yourself with the essence of spellfire, gaining the following benefits while this use of Innate Sorcery is active. 

**Burning Life Force**: Once per turn when you are hit by an attack roll, you can expend up to $(Math.max(1,stats.cha.mod)) Hit Point Dice. Roll the expended dice, and reduce the amount of damage from that attack equal to the total rolled.

**Flight**: You gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover.

**Spell Avoidance**: When you’re subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the save and only half damage if you fail. You can’t use this benefit if you have the Incapacitated condition.

name: Wild Magic Sorcery
description: Your innate magic stems from the forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation. You or an ancestor might have endured exposure to raw magic, perhaps through a planar portal leading to Limbo or the Elemental Planes. Perhaps you were blessed by a fey being or marked by a demon. Or your magic could be a fluke with no apparent cause. Whatever its source, this magic churns within you, waiting for any outlet.

children.name: Wild Magic Surge
children.description: Your spellcasting unleashes surges of untamed magic.Once per turn, roll 1d20 immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot. If you roll a 20, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table.

children.name: Tides of Chaos
children.description:
You manipulate chaos itself to give yourself Advantage on one D20 Test before you roll.
Once you do so, you must cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot or finish a Long Rest before you can use this feature again.

If you do cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot before you finish a Long Rest, you automatically roll on the Wild Magic Surge table.

children.name: Bend Luck
children.description: You twist fate using your wild magic. Immediately after another creature you can see rolls the d20 for a D20 Test, roll 1d4 and apply the number rolled as a bonus or penalty (your choice) to the d20 roll.

children.name: Controlled Chaos
children.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, roll twice and use either number.

children.name: Tamed Surge
children.description: Immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot, you can create an effect of your choice from the Wild Magic Surge table instead of rolling on that table. You can choose any effect in the table except for the final row.

name: Archfey Patron
description: Your pact draws on the power of the Feywild. When you choose this subclass, you might make a deal with an archfey, such as the Prince of Frost; the Queen of Air and Darkness, ruler of the Gloaming Court; Titania of the Summer Court; or an ancient hag.

overwrite.extra:
$(meta.level >= 6 ? "**Misty Escape** You can cast this spell as a Reaction in response to taking damage." : "")

In addition, you can choose one of the following additional effects:

**Refreshing Step.** You or a creature within 10 feet of yourself gains 1d10 Temporary Hit Points.

**Taunting Step.** Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on attacks against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.

$(meta.level >= 6 ? "**Disappearing Step.** You have the invisible condition until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you attack or cast a spell." : "")

$(meta.level >= 6 ? "**Dreadful Step.** Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left or the space you appear in must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or take 2d10 Psychic damage." : "")

children.name: Beguiling Defenses
children.description: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, reduce the damage you take by half (round down), and force the attacker to make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the attacker takes Psychic damage equal to the damage you take.

children.name: Bewitching Magic
children.description: Immediately after you cast this spell, you can cast Misty Step as part of the same action without expending a slot.

name: Celestial Patron
description: Your pact draws on the Upper Planes, the realms of everlasting bliss. You might enter an agreement with an empyrean, a couatl, a sphinx, a unicorn, or another heavenly entity. Or you might call on numerous such beings as you pursue goals aligned with theirs.

children.name: Healing Light
children.description: Heal yourself or a creature you can see. The target regains 1d6 Hit Points per charge expeded, up to a maximum of $(Math.min(1, stats.cha.mod))d6 Hit Points.

children.target: spell AND damageSpell AND (radiantDamage OR fireDamage)
children.name: Radiant Soul
children.description: Once per turn, add $(stats.cha.mod) to one damage roll.

children.name: Celestial Resilience
children.description: You gain $(meta.level + stats.cha.mod) Temporary Hit Points. Choose up to 5 creatures. They gain $(Math.floor(meta.level / 2) + stats.cha.mod) Temporary Hit Points.

children.name: Searing Vengeance
children.description:
When you or an ally within range of you is about to make a Death Saving Throw, you unleash radiant energy to save the creature. The creature regains Hit Points equal to half its Hit Point maximum and can end the Prone condition on itself. 

Each creature of your choice that is within 30 feet of the creature takes Radiant damage equal to 2d8$(formatBonus(stats.cha.mod)) and has the Blinded condition until the end of the current turn.

name: Fiend Patron
description: Your pact draws on the Lower Planes, the realms of perdition. You might forge a bargain with a demon lord such as Demogorgon or Orcus; an archdevil such as Asmodeus; or a pit fiend.

children.name: Dark One's Blessing
children.description: When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain $(meta.level + stats.cha.mod) Temporary Hit Points. You also gain this benefit if someone else reduces an enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 Hit Points.

children.name: Dark One's Own Luck
children.description: You call on your fiendish patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add 1d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the roll but before any of the roll’s effects occur.

children.name: Fiendish Resilience
children.description: Choose one damage type (other than Force). You have Resistance to it.

children.name: Hurl Through Hell
children.description:
Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you try to instantly transport the target through the Lower Planes. The target must succeed on DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Charisma saving throw, or the target disappears and hurtles through a nightmare landscape. 

The target takes 8d10 Psychic damage if it isn’t a Fiend, and it has the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn, when it returns to the space it previously occupied or the nearest unoccupied space.

name: Great Old One Patron
description: Your pact draws on the Far Realm or an elder god—a being such as Tharizdun, Hadar, or Great Cthulhu. The motives of these beings are incomprehensible, and the Great Old One might be indifferent to your existence.

children.name: Awakened Mind
children.description: You form a telepathic connection between your mind and the mind of another. As a Bonus Action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You and the chosen creature can communicate telepathically with each other while the two of you are within $(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod)) miles of each other. To understand each other, you each must mentally use a language the other knows.

children.target: warlock AND damageSpell NOT (enchantment OR illusion)
children.name: Psychic Spells
children.description: You can change the damage type to Psychic.

children.name: Psychic Spells
children.description: You can cast this spell without Verbal or Somatic components.

children.target: warlock AND (enchantment OR illusion) AND damageSpell
children.name: Psychic Spells
children.description: You can cast this spell without Verbal or Somatic components and change the damage type to Psychic.

children.name: Clairvoyant Combatant
children.description: When you form a telepathic bond with a creature using your Awakened Mind, you force that creature to make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you, and you have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature for the duration of the bond.

children.name: Eldritch Hex
children.description: The target has Disadvantage on Saving Throws of the chosen ability.

children.name: Thought Shield
children.description: When a creature deals Psychic damage to you, that creature takes the same amount of damage that you take.

children.name: Create Thrall
children.description:
You can modify this spell so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting, and when summoned, the Aberration has $(meta.level + stats.cha.mod) Temporary Hit Points.

The first time each turn the Aberration hits a creature under the effect of your Hex, the Aberration deals extra Psychic damage to the target equal to the bonus damage of that spell.

name: Abjurer
description: Your study of magic is focused on spells that block, banish, or protect—ending harmful effects, banishing evil influences, and protecting the weak. Abjurers are sought when baleful spirits require exorcism, when locations must be guarded against magical spying, and when portals to other planes of existence must be closed.

children.name: Arcane Ward
children.description:
When you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, you weave magic around yourself for protection. You simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a ward with $(2 * meta.level + stats.int.mod) Hit Points that lasts until you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead, and if you have any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward’s Hit Points. If the damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 Hit Points, it can’t absorb damage, but its magic remains. 

Whenever you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot. Alternatively, as a Bonus Action, you can expend a spell slot, and the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot expended.

children.name: Projected Ward
children.description: When a creature that you can see within range takes damage, you cause your Arcane Ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, the warded creature takes any remaining damage. If that creature has any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward's Hit Points.

children.name: Spell Breaker
children.description: The spell slot isn't expended if the spell fails to stop a spell.

name: Bladesinger
description:
Bladesingers master a tradition of wizardry that incorporates swordplay and dance. In combat, a Bladesinger uses intricate, elegant maneuvers that fend off harm and allow the Bladesinger to channel magic into devastating attacks and a cunning defense.

Bladesinging is associated with the ancient elven societies that first mastered the art and coined the term. Even today, most Bladesingers still hail from old elven realms, such as Myth Drannor, or from non-elven societies that share land and history with elves, such as the Silver Marches.

children.name: Bladesong
children.description:
You invoke an elven magic called the Bladesong. While the Bladesong is active, you gain the following benefits:

**Agility.** You gain a +$(Math.max(1, stats.int.mod)) bonus to your AC, and your Speed increased by 10 feet. You also have Advantage on Acrobatics.

**Bladework.** You make attack rolls with a $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod+attributes.prof)) modifier and a $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)) damage bonus.

**Focus.** When you make a Constitution saving throw to maintain Concentration, you can add $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)) to the total.

children.name: Bladesong
children.description: You regain one charge of Bladesong.

children.name: Bladesinger Skill Proficiency
children.target: acrobaticsProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR performanceProficiency OR persuasionProficiency

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. You can replace one attack with a cantrip that has a casting time of an action.

children.name: Song of Defense
children.description: When you take damage while your Bladesong is active, you reduce the damage taken by an amount equal to five times the spell slot’s level.

children.name: Song of Victory
children.target: (level1Spell OR level2Spell OR level3Spell OR level4Spell OR level5Spell OR level6Spell OR level7Spell OR level8Spell OR level9Spell) AND time=action
children.description: Make one attack with a weapon.

name: Diviner
description: The counsel of a Diviner is sought by those who want a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a Diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness. You work to master spells of discernment, remote viewing, supernatural knowledge, and foresight.

children.name: Portent
children.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, glimpses on the future press on your awareness. Roll $(meta.level >= 14 ? '3' : '2')d20s and record the numbers rolled. You can replace any D20 Test made by you or a creature that you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn.

children.name: Expert Divination
children.description: You regain an expended spell slot. The spell slot can be of a level lower than the slot you used, but no higher than 5th level.

children.name: The Third Eye
children.description:
You increase your powers of perception. Choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you start a Short or Long Rest.

**Darkvision.** You gain Darkvision with a range of 120 feet.

**Greater Comprehension.** You can read any language.

**See Invisibility.** You can cast See Invisibility without expending a spell slot.

name: Evoker
description: Your studies focus on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid. Some Evokers find employment in military forces, serving as artillery to blast armies from afar. Others use their power to protect others, while some seek their own gain.

children.name: Potent Cantrip
children.description: If the target succeeds on a saving throw against this cantrip, it takes half damage but suffers no additional effect.

children.name: Sculpt Spells
children.description: When you cast this spell, your allies automatically succeed on their saving throw and take no damage if they would normally take half.

children.name: Empowered Evocation
children.description: Add $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)) to one damage roll of the spell.

children.name: Overchannel
children.description:
When you cast a level 1-5 Wizard spell that deals damage, you increase its power to deal maximum damage.
The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before you finish a Long Rest, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity.

name: Illusionist
description: You specialize in magic that dazzles the senses and tricks the mind, and the illusions you craft make the impossible seem real. Most Wizards believe that their counterparts in ancient civilizations knew secrets of magic that have been lost to the ages, and discovering those secrets could unlock the path to a power greater than any magic available in the present age.

children.name: Improved Illusions
children.description: You can cast this spell without Verbal components. If the spell has a range of 10+ feet, the range increases by 60 feet.

children.name: Illusory Self
children.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you interpose an illusory duplicate of yourself between the attacker and yourself. The attack automatically misses, then the illusion dissipates.

children.name: Illusory Reality
children.description: You weave magic into illusions to give them a semi-reality. Choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of an Illusion spell you cast. Make it real for the duration.

name: Warlock
description: Warlocks quest for knowledge that lies hidden in the fabric of the multiverse. They often begin their search for magical power by delving into tomes of forbidden lore, dabbling in invocations meant to attract the power of extraplanar beings, or seeking places of power where the influence of these beings can be felt. In no time, each Warlock is drawn into a binding pact with a powerful patron. Drawing on the ancient knowledge of beings such as angels, archfey, demons, devils, hags, and alien entities of the Far Realm, Warlocks piece together arcane secrets to bolster their own power.

children.name: Warlock Skill Proficiencies
children.target: arcanaProficiency OR deceptionProficiency OR historyProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR investigationProficiency OR natureProficiency OR religionProficiency

children.name: Warlock Prepared Spells
children.target: warlock NOT level9Spell NOT level8Spell NOT level7Spell NOT level6Spell NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

children.name: Magical Cunning
children.description: You perform an esoteric rite. At the end of it, you $(meta.level >= 20 ? "regain all your expended Pact Magic spell slots." : "regain expended Pact Magic spell slots equal to half your maximum (round up).")

children.name: Contact Patron
children.description: You automatically succeed on the spell's saving throw. Once you cast this spell, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

name: Wizard
description:
Wizards are defined by their exhaustive study of magic's inner workings. They cast spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception, and spectacular transformations. Their magic conjures monsters from other planes of existence, glimpses the future, or forms protective barriers. Their mightiest spells change one substance into another, call meteors from the sky, or open portals to other worlds.

Most Wizards share a scholarly approach to magic. They examine the theoretical underpinnings of magic, particularly the categorization of spells into schools of magic. Renowned Wizards such as Bigby, Tasha, Mordenkainen, and Yolande have built on their studies to invent iconic spells now used across the multiverse.

children.name: Wizard Skill Proficiencies
children.target: arcanaProficiency OR historyProficiency OR insightProficiency OR investigationProficiency OR medicineProficiency OR natureProficiency OR religionProficiency

children.name: Wizard Prepared Spells
children.target: wizard NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

variables.maxLevel: $(meta.level >= 17 ? 9 : meta.level >= 15 ? 8 : meta.level >= 13 ? 7 : meta.level >= 11 ? 6 : meta.level >= 9 ? 5 : meta.level >= 7 ? 4 : meta.level >= 5 ? 3 : meta.level >= 3 ? 2 : 1)

children.name: Arcane Recovery
children.description: When you finish a Short Rest, you can recover expended spell slots. The slots can have a combined level equal to no more than $(Math.ceil(meta.level / 2)) and none can be level 6 or higher.

children.name: Scholar Skill Expertise
children.target: arcanaExpertise OR historyExpertise OR insightExpertise OR investigationExpertise OR medicineExpertise OR natureExpertise OR religionExpertise

overwrite.extra: **Spell Mastery.** You can cast this spell at will without expending a spell slot.
**Signature Spell.** You can cast this spell without expending a spell slot once per Long Rest.

children.name: Dash
children.description: You gain extra movement for the current turn. Your Speed increases by $(attributes.movement.walk).

children.name: Disengage
children.description: Your movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks for the rest of the current turn.

children.name: Hide
children.description: You try to conceal yourself while you're Heavily Obscured or behind Three-Quarters Cover or Total Cover. Make a $(formatBonus(skills.stealth.bonus)) DC 15 Dexterity (Stealth) check. On a succesful check, you have the Invisible condition until you make a sound louder than a whisper, an enemy finds you, you make an attack roll, or you cast a spell with a Verbal component.

children.name: Dodge
children.description: Any attack roll made against you has Disadvantage if you can see the attacker, and you make Dexterity saving throws with Advantage.

children.name: Opportunity Attack
children.description: When a creature leaves your reach, you make one melee attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike against the target. The attack occurs right before the creature leaves your reach.

children.name: Study
children.description:
You make an Intelligence check to study your memory, a book, a clue, or another source of knowledge and call to mind an important piece of information about it.

**Arcana ($(formatBonus(skills.arcana.bonus))).** Spells, magic items, eldritch symbols, magical traditions, planes of existence, and certain creatures (Aberrations, Constructs, Elementals, Fey, and Monstrosities)

**History ($(formatBonus(skills.history.bonus))).** Historic events and people, ancient civilizations, wars, and certain creatures (Giants and Humanoids)

**Investigation ($(formatBonus(skills.investigation.bonus))).** Traps, ciphers, riddles, and gadgetry

**Nature ($(formatBonus(skills.nature.bonus))).** Terrain, flora, weather, and certain creatures (Beasts, Dragons, Oozes, and Plants)

**Religion ($(formatBonus(skills.religion.bonus))).** Deities, religious hierarchies and rites, holy symbols, cults, and certain creatures (Celestials, Fiends, and Undead)

children.name: Search
children.description:
You make a Wisdom check to discern something that isn't obvious. 

**Insight ($(formatBonus(skills.insight.bonus))).** Creature's state of mind

**Medicine ($(formatBonus(skills.medicine.bonus))).** Creature's ailment or cause of death

**Perception ($(formatBonus(skills.perception.bonus))).** Concealed creature or object

**Survival ($(formatBonus(skills.survival.bonus))).** Tracks or food

children.name: Influence
children.description:
You make a Wisdom or Charisma check to urge a creature or group of creatures to do something. 

**Deception ($(formatBonus(skills.deception.bonus))).** Lie

**Intimidation ($(formatBonus(skills.intimidation.bonus))).** Threaten

**Performance ($(formatBonus(skills.performance.bonus))).** Entertain

**Persuasion ($(formatBonus(skills.persuasion.bonus))).** Persuade

**Animal Handling ($(formatBonus(skills.animal_handling.bonus))).** Control or train a Beast

children.name: Help
children.description:
You do one of the following.

**Assist an Ability Check.** Choose one of your skill or tool proficiencies and one ally who is near enough for you to assist verbally or physically when they make an ability check. That ally has Advantage on the next ability check they make with the chosen skill or tool. This benefit expires if the ally doesn't use it before the start of your next turn. The DM has final say on whether your assistance is possible.

**Assist an Attack Roll.** You momentarily distract an enemy within 5 feet of you, giving Advantage to the next attack roll by one of your allies against that enemy. This benefit expires at the start of your next turn.

children.name: Ready
children.description:
You take the Ready action to wait for a particular circumstance before you act. To do so, you take this action on your turn, which lets you act by taking a Reaction before the start of your next turn.

First, you decide what perceivable circumstance will trigger your Reaction. Then, you choose the action you will take in response to that trigger, or you choose to move up to your Speed in response to it. Examples include "If the cultist steps on the trapdoor, I'll pull the lever that opens it," and "If the zombie steps next to me, I move away."

When the trigger occurs, you can either take your Reaction right after the trigger finishes or ignore the trigger.

name: Long Rest
description:
A Long Rest is a period of extended downtime—at least 8 hours—available to any creature. During a Long Rest, you sleep for at least 6 hours and perform no more than 2 hours of light activity, such as reading, talking, eating, or standing watch.
During sleep, you have the Unconscious condition. After you finish a Long Rest, you must wait at least 16 hours before starting another one.
You regain all lost Hit Points and all spent Hit Point Dice.

name: Short Rest
description:
A Short Rest is a 1-hour period of downtime, during which a creature does nothing more strenuous than reading, talking, eating, or standing watch. To start a Short Rest, you must have at least 1 Hit Point.

**Spend Hit Point Dice.** You can spend one or more of your Hit Point Dice to regain Hit Points. For each Hit Point Die you spend in this way, you regain 1d$(attributes.hitDie)$(formatBonus(stats.con.mod)) Hit Points. You can decide to spend an additional Hit Point Die after each roll.

name: Attack: $(local.name)
description: You strike with your weapon. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes[local.classification].attack)) attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(local.damageRoll)$(formatBonus(attributes[local.classification].bonus)) $(capitalize(local.damageType)) damage.

name: Attack: Unarmed Strike
description: You strike with a punch, kick, head-butt or similar forceful blow. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.melee.attack)) attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d4$(formatBonus(attributes.melee.bonus)) Bludgeoning damage.

children.name: Ritual
children.description: You can cast this spell as a Ritual instead. The Ritual version of a spell takes 10 minutes longer to cast, and doesn't expend a Spell Slot.

children.name: Light
children.description: You can make an additional attack with a Light weapon as a Bonus Action. You do not add your Ability Modifier to the attack's damage roll.

children.name: Graze
children.description: On a miss, deal $(attributes[local.classification].bonus) $(local.damageType) damage.

children.name: Slow
children.description: On a hit, the target's speed is reduced by 10 feet.

children.name: Nick
children.description: You can make the additional attack of the Light property as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action.
On a hit, you can make another attack against a second creature within 5 feet of the first that is also within your reach.

children.name: Push
children.description: On a hit, push the target up to 10 feet straight away from yourself.

children.name: Topple
children.description: On a hit, the target must succeed a DC $(8 + attributes.prof + attributes[local.classification].bonus) Constitution saving throw or have the Prone condition.

children.name: Sap
children.description: On a hit, the target has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

children.name: Vex
children.description: On a hit, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against the target before the end of your next turn.

children.name: Grapple or Shove
children.description: You can replace one attack with a grapple or shove attempt. The target must succeed on a DC $(8+attributes.prof+Math.max(stats.str.mod, stats.dex.mod)) Dexterity saving throw or it has the Grappled or Prone condition.

children.name: Peerless Aim
children.description: On a miss, you can hit instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Blink Steps
children.target: weaponAttack OR cantrip OR level1Spell OR level2Spell OR level3Spell OR level4Spell OR level5Spell OR level6Spell OR level7Spell OR level8Spell OR level9Spell OR level9Spell
children.description: You can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

children.name: Energy Resistances
children.target: acidResistance OR coldResistance OR fireResistance OR lightningResistance OR necroticResistance OR poisonResistance OR psychicResistance OR radiantResistance OR thunderResistance OR epicBoonResistance

children.name: Energy Redirection
children.description: When you take damage of a type you have Resistance to, you can direct damage of the same type toward another creature you can see within range that isn't behind Total Cover. That creature must succeed on a DC $(8 + stats.con.mod + attributes.prof) Dexterity saving throw or take 2d12$(formatBonus(stats.con.mod)) damage.

children.name: Improve Fate
children.description: When you or another creature within range succeeds on or fails a D20 Test, you can roll 2d4 and apply the total rolled as a bonus or penalty to the d20 roll.

children.name: Fortified Health
children.description: Whenever you regain Hit Points, you can regain additional $(stats.con.mod) Hit Points.

children.name: Overcome Defenses
children.description: This damage ignores Resistance.

children.name: Overwhelming Strike
children.description: On a 20, the target takes an extra $(Math.max(stats.str.mod, stats.dex.mod)) damage.

children.name: Last Stand
children.description: When you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead and regain $(Math.floor(attributes.hp / 2)) Hit Points.

children.name: Recover Vitality
children.description: Regain 1d10 Hit Points per charge expended.

children.name: Escape Artist
children.description: You take the Disengage action and also end the Grappled condition on you.

children.name: Free Casting
children.target: level1Spell OR level2Spell OR level3Spell OR level4Spell
children.description: Roll 1d4. If the number you roll is the same as the slot's level, the slot isn't expended.

children.name: Merge with Shadows
children.description: While within Dim Light or Darkness, you give yourself the Invisible condition. The condition ends on you immediately after you take an action, a Bonus Action, or a Reaction. Additionally, you have Resistance to all damage except Psychic and Radiant.

name: Great Weapon Fighting
description: On a hit, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3.

name: Interception
description: When a creature you can see hits another creature with an attack roll, you reduce the damage dealt to the target by 1d10+$(attributes.prof).

name: Protection
description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you, you impose Disadvantage on the triggering attack roll and all other attack rolls against the target until the start of your next turn.

target: activities[weaponAttack].extra["You do not add your Ability Modifier to the attack's damage roll."]
name: Two Weapon Fighting

children.name: Unarmed Fighting
children.description: At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 Bludgeoning damage to one creature grappled by you.

children.name: Actor
children.description: While you're disguised as a real or fictional person, you have Advantage on Charisma (Deception or Performance) checks to convince others that you are that person. Additionally, you can mimic the sounds of other creatures, including speech. A creature that hears the mimicry must succeed on a DC $(8 + stats.cha.mod + attributes.prof) Wisdom (Insight) check to determine the effect is faked.

children.name: Improved Dash
children.description: Your Speed increases by 10 feet for that action.

children.name: Charge
children.description: If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line toward the target immediately before hitting it, choose one of the following effects — gain a 1d8 bonus to the attack's damage roll, or push the target up to 10 feet away if it is no more than one size larger than you.

children.name: Replenishing Meal
children.description: You can cook special food if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand. You can prepare enough of this food for $(4 + attributes.prof) creatures. Any creature who eats the food and spends one or more Hit Dice to regain Hit Points regains an extra 1d8 Hit Points.

children.name: Bolstering Treats
children.description: With 1 hour of work or when you finish a Long Rest, you cook a number of treats equal to $(attributes.prof) if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand. These special treats last 8 hours after being made. A creature can use a Bonus Action to eat one of those treats to gain $(attributes.prof) Temporary Hit Points.

children.target: activities[weaponAttack].extra["You do not add your Ability Modifier to the attack's damage roll"].description

children.name: Crusher
children.description: On a hit, you can move the target 5 feet to an unoccupied space if the target is no more than one size larger than you.

children.name: Enhanced Critical
children.description: On a Critical Hit, attack rolls against the target have Advantage until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Parry
children.description: When you are hit with an attack, you add $(attributes.prof) to your Armor Class, potentially causing the attack to miss you. You gain this bonus to your AC against melee attacks until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Speedy Recovery
children.description: You can expend one of your Hit Dice, roll the die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll.

children.name: Energy Mastery
children.target: acidResistance OR coldResistance OR fireResistance OR lightningResistance or thunderResistance OR elementalAdeptFeatResistance

children.name: Attack Advantage
children.description: You have Advantage on attack rolls against a creature Grappled by you.

children.name: Fast Wrestler
children.description: You don't have to spend extra movement to move a creature Grappled by you if the creature is your size or smaller.

children.name: Grapple
